title: hieracium murorum subsp. subplacerum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. subplacerum, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Hieracium murorum subsp. subplacerum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture, tea, or salve to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
